mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS path signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so a falling policy rate over the recent month predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20d SPX returns do not occur more often when FEDFUNDS is falling than when it is flat or rising, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates and FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real rates compress and the market interprets policy as behind the curve, which should support equity multiples and positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS is below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes fail to deliver above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign of the return response becomes negative when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS move together.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should pressure sales expectations and compress equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not worse on months with rising UNRATE than on months with falling or stable UNRATE, or if the signal's 3-year rolling hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS decline paired with a higher UNRATE confirms a labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption and earnings expectations, so SPX should fall over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp within 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up trigger does not precede negative 5d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in RSAFS reflects resilient retail demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not align with above-median 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional rank correlation is indistinguishable from zero over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST indicates housing-demand strength and a broader wealth-effect impulse that supports cyclicals and the index, so rising housing starts should predict positive 60-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts surges are not followed by positive excess 60d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal becomes negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO together with stable or falling UNRATE signals a supply-side expansion without labor stress, which should support earnings expectations and positive SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while UNRATE does not increase
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up/UNRATE-not-up condition does not beat unconditional 20d returns, or if a positive INDPRO reading alone explains the effect without UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 combined with weak PAYEMS marks a broad real-activity contraction that should reduce forward earnings estimates and produce negative SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative and PAYEMS growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if recession-like GDPC1/PAYEMS contractions do not precede negative 60d SPX returns more often than chance, or if returns remain positive in most triggered instances.
